title: Classic British Lamb Roast
description: A traditional British-style lamb roast recipe, perfect for a special occasion. This recipe serves 4-6 people and is a hearty, flavorful dish.

ingredients.name: Lamb Roast (2-3 pounds)
ingredients.notes: ask your butcher for a bone-in leg of lamb

instructions: Rinse the lamb roast and pat it dry with paper towels.
Season the lamb with salt, black pepper, and garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the lamb to a large roasting pan and add the rosemary sprigs.
Roast the lamb in the preheated oven for 20 minutes per pound, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
While the lamb is roasting, toss the carrots and potatoes with a little bit of oil and season with salt and pepper.
After the lamb has been roasting for 30 minutes, add the carrots and potatoes to the roasting pan.
Continue roasting for another 30-40 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the lamb reaches the desired level of doneness.
Remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve the lamb with the roasted carrots and potatoes, and garnish with fresh rosemary.
